scenarios.title: Core reflects the ML sidecar's model_loaded:false instead of fabricating true

gherkin.given: the ML sidecar's GET /health returns 200 with {"status":"up","model_loaded":false} (the live prod condition the redteam observed)
gherkin.when: core's checkMLSidecar probes the sidecar and builds the services.ml_sidecar aggregate entry
gherkin.then: core reports model_loaded:false (reflected from the sidecar body), never the fabricated true (adversarial: fails against the pre-fix hardcode loaded:=true)

scenarios.title: Core reflects the ML sidecar's self-declared degraded status

gherkin.given: the ML sidecar's GET /health returns 200 with {"status":"degraded","model_loaded":false}
gherkin.when: core's checkMLSidecar probes the sidecar and builds the aggregate entry
gherkin.then: core reports status:degraded reflected from the sidecar body rather than overriding it to up, so a self-declared degraded sidecar surfaces into the aggregate

scenarios.title: Core does not fabricate a model_loaded claim on a reachable-but-unparseable body

gherkin.given: the ML sidecar returns 200 with an empty or unparseable body (reachable but unknown)
gherkin.when: core's checkMLSidecar probes the sidecar and builds the aggregate entry
gherkin.then: core reports status:up with NO model_loaded claim (nil), never a fabricated value — the anti-fabrication half of the fix

scenarios.title: The ML sidecar's /health reports the embedder's live model_loaded, not a stale import snapshot

gherkin.given: the ML sidecar module app.main was imported while embedder._model was None and the model is loaded later on the first generate_embedding()
gherkin.when: GET /health is served after the lazy load reassigns embedder._model
gherkin.then: the sidecar reports model_loaded:true via embedder.is_model_loaded() (call-time read), never the frozen import-time false (adversarial: fails against the pre-fix from .embedder import _model binding)
